texto: A fire, if it is large enough, is not easily contained. Sparks fly out, and the wind carries them in all directions. Like its brothers, the fire...in Mirusia’s heart spewed forth sparks, and, without her consciously realizing what was happening, they began to ignite that which had no reason to be burned.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Monika Barbara Potocki: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “A fire, if it is large enough, is not easily contained. Sparks fly out, and the wind carries them…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, brothers, burned pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de burned e fire.
